source_url: https://aaboard.org/cases/Afterschool Mini AI Summer Camp K-2/AAB-CASE-2025-LL-002.html
summary: Afterschool summer camp (5 days) using a generative AI storytelling application plus AI concept slides with learners ages ~5–7 in Diamond Bar, Southern California. The activity emphasized creative storytelling and early AI awareness, with post-assessment included.

implementing_organization.primary_facilitator_role: Undergrad. and graduate CS students; technical educators

learner_profile.prior_ai_exposure: No prior experience with generative AI tools assumed

secondary_learning_goals: Awareness of AI as a tool (not a human)
Reflection on human vs AI contributions

ai_tool.tool_type: Generative AI storytelling application; AI concept slides
user_interaction_model: Students pick their favorite stuffed animals
Educators take a picture of the animal
Students provide or pick short prompts using text or voice
Students provide or pick more prompts using text or voice

observed_challenges: One student cannot read or speak English
Some prompts were vague or contradictory
Younger students needed help typing prompts
One student felt not learned, so he was later added to the age 8-10 group.
design_adaptations: Added pause points for human editing

engagement: Peer discussion increased after AI output
reported_outcomes.educators_reflection: It was surprising and deeply encouraging to see that a student who does not yet read or speak English was still able to learn core AI concepts and perform very well on the assessment quiz. This experience reinforced how AI learning—when designed with visuals, interaction, and hands-on exploration—can be inclusive and accessible beyond language barriers.
